entry.title: Automate Job Info Portlet with Position to Job Info Synchronization in SAP SF Employee Central
entry.content:
Agenda

  1. Why Synchronization Position and Job Info fields? in SAP Successfactors Employee Central.
  2. How Synchronization works in SAP Successfactors Employee Central.
  3. How to configure Position → JobInfo sync using Business Rules in SAP Successfactors Employee Central.
  4. Pro-tips for error reduction and automation.
  5. Final thoughts.

Why Should Position and Job Info Be Synced?

  • In SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central, both Position and Job Information Portlets often hold overlapping data → like Department, Location, or Division. When these don’t match, confusion and manual errors occur.
  • By enabling Position-to-Job Info Synchronization , we allow Automatic Population of updated values from the position object into the employee’s job information portlet. It saves time and avoids discrepancies
  • Position and job information portlet Both should Match. Usually positions have tendency to Synchronize data to job information portlet. That means if you get 1 new update in position then those information can be automatically populated from Position job information portlet Info Via position to Job information Synchronization.
  • If you have different information on employee profile i .e job information portlet and on a position, how can you recognize the correct information?
  • So, You need to synced both Position and job information portlet data to avoid mismatch inofrmation to reduced the inconsistencey in the data. If you are not using Synchronization then you're losing automation as the data from the position is not matched with Job Information portlet.
  • In configuration using position management, only use Position-to-Job Info synchronization most commonly used the other Synchronization is a  little bit tricky and is not as much straight forward as this Positio-To-Job Information. I strongly recommend you to use Position to Job Information Synchronization in Manage Position management settings to reduce this data redundancy.

How Synchronization works:

  • Go to "Position Management Settings".
  • Navigate to the "Synchronization Tab".
    • The "Synchronization Tab" determines the behavior of the system when synchronizing data from a Position to an employee’s Job Information. This includes the rules for defining fields used in synchronization of Position to an employee’s Job Information.
  • Configure the fields that should sync
  • Add Business Rules for triggering updates (e.g., when a position is changed, reflect changes in Job Info).

How to configure Position → JobInfo sync using Business Rules in SAP SF EC:

To configure Position → JobInfo synchronization using Business Rules in SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central, follow this step-by-step guide, specifically using the "Synchronize Position Changes to Incumbents" scenario.

Step-by-Step: Position to JobInfo Sync Configuration with Business Rule:

Define the Use Case:

  • When a change happens in the Position object (like Department, Division, Location), it should automatically reflect in the employee's Job Information portlet.
  • This is handled via a Business Rule using the Scenario: Synchronize Position Changes to Incumbents.

Create the Business Rule:

  1. Go to Configure Business Rules.

  2. Click Create New Rule.

  3. Choose Position Management  - Pre Delivered Template. 

  4. Under Scenario, select: Synchronize Position Changes to Incumbents.

Register the Rule in Position Management Settings:

  1. Go to Admin Center → Position Management Settings.

  2. Navigate to the Synchronization Tab.

  3. Find the field: "Rule for Synchronizing Position to Job Information".

  4. Assign the Business Rule you just created (Sync_Position_To_JobInfo)

  5. Save the configuration.

Ensure Sync Triggers on Job Info Changes:

  • Also make sure:

    • If the position field is edited within Job Info, this rule should still trigger.

    • Use Position Code as a field in Job Information portlet.

    • Validate permissions and ensure the employee is tied to a valid position.

Pro-tips for error reduction and automation:

  • Use only the direction Position → JobInfo Synchronization.
  • Avoid two-way Synchronization i.e. Position → JobInfo Synchronization & JobInfo → Position Synchronization. to reduce data loops and overwrites.
  • "If you're not syncing the data then there is mandatory inconsistency between both Position & Job Information Portlet, you're missing automation."
  • Test in Preview or Staging before deploying to Production.
  • Clearly document synced fields to reduce confusion.

Final Thoughts on Position → Job Information Synchronization:

Synchronizing between Position to Job Information portlet is not just a technical configuration—it's a strategic step toward ensuring data integrity, process automation, and organizational consistency.

By automating this Synchronization:

  • You eliminate manual data entry and human errors.

  • Keep employee records aligned with organizational structures.

  • Enable clean, audit-ready data for downstream processes like reporting, payroll, and workflows.

When configured correctly using business rules and one-way sync, it transforms how your HR operations function—bringing accuracy, efficiency, and confidence to every transaction.

Regards,

Mahnoor Naseem

SAP SuccessFactors Consultant

If you found this blog a useful please consider giving it a Kudos

entry.title: Re: send mail delayed
entry.content:
You could try the following but you'd also have to put in work to generate the timestamp for a future date/time and the domain definition suggests UTC.  Please note that I believe the normal way would be to call the SEND_SCREEN method which appears to allow a user to input such information.

  DATA: email    TYPE REF TO cl_bcs,
        exc      TYPE REF TO cx_bcs.

* Generate one in the future for true delay
  GET TIME STAMP FIELD DATA(timestamp).

  TRY.
*   Generate send request
    email = cl_bcs=>create_persistent( ).
    email->send_request->set_send_at( timestamp ).

*   Create and set document
    ...

*   Create sender and recipient addresses
    ...

*   Send document
    DATA(sent) = email->send( ).
    IF sent IS NOT INITIAL.
      COMMIT WORK.
    ELSE.
      ROLLBACK WORK.
    ENDIF.

    CATCH cx_bcs INTO exc.
*     No email sent - do nothing here
  ENDTRY.

 

Regards,
Ryan Crosby

entry.title: Re: Unable to create notification in SAP Asset Manager
entry.content: Issued this couple times in different versions of SSAM. The problem in my case was in priorities of Notification type. So when you use M1 it is standard and there are records in table T356 for standard priority type PM assigned to M1 notification type. But when you try to use custom Notif Type you configure custom priority type that doesn`t have priorities in T356 and there is an issue. So do correct configuration adding priorities to your custom priority type and this should solve the problem. Hope it helps.

entry.title: Re: EDI Setup for Billing Document - S4 HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello Asesh,

Thank you for the clarification and for sharing the details. Based on your message and the screenshot, the root cause of the issue is clear

“The system is missing an active Communication Arrangement for the Billing Document output via EDI”.

  • For pure EDI scenarios (Channel = EDI), the Template field in Output Parameter Determination is optional. You only assign a Template if a formatted output (example PDF) is required.
  • Your error message confirms that an active Communication Arrangement is required for the Billing Document output to be processed successfully.

what to do,

Maintain an active Communication Arrangement for EDI with scenario

  • SAP_COM_0440 – Sales Integration (Outbound)

Ensure your Output Parameter Determination settings include:

    • Output Type: BILLING_DOCUMENT
    • Channel: EDI
    • Receiver: Correctly maintained
    • Template: Can be left blank for standard EDI

Useful SAP Reference

  • Communication Arrangements – SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Once the Communication Arrangement is properly maintained, the EDI output will trigger as expected.

Let me know if you need more support

With kind regards

Chuma

SAP Customer Engagement Initiative gives you the chance to work directly with SAP product managers and developers, offering your feedback on ideas, prototypes, and plans while they’re still in early stages.

To help you explore the available projects, we’ve compiled a detailed list for your convenience. Check out the full list here or follow the links below to discover the exciting opportunities:

SAP S/4HANA Cloud

Cloud Delivery & Excellence

Digital Supply Chain

Private Cloud

Public Cloud

Let’s shape the future together!

entry.title: Re: Project System work in progress accrued income
entry.content:
A couple of points:

1. To accrue WIP you should be doing T&M Billing using RRB.  The RA method you show is 3 which is Cost Based POC.  For T&M billing the RA methods are 14 or 15.  With these RA will accrue revenue for unbilled incurred costs.  When you run RA it looks like RRB.

2. In the RA run you showed you have already invoiced 6M which is your total planned revenue and posted 100% of your planned costs.  So there is nothing left to accrue.

3.  If you do want Cost Based POC then try another test project but don't invoice anything or just partial.  And incur some costs just not all of the plan to see how it works.

entry.title: Microsoft AutoGen v0.6.1 with SAP AI Core
entry.content:
Introduction

As a part of Intelligent Product Recommendation team we are collaborating with Microsoft to use Autogen for agentic usecases. I initially came across a helpful post AutoGen with SAP AI Core by @lars_gregori , which explores the integration of autogen using pyautogen and llm_config. However, use of llm_config is deprecated in v0.6.1 of AutoGen.  This blog will guide you how to integrate AutoGen  with SAP AI Core with the help of  AzureOpenAIChatCompletionClient .

Background

Currently, IPR product is already using python generative-ai-hub-sdk to call the LLM which takes in the 'AI-Resource-Group' as the required header and the model name. Now we have came accross a critical business usecase that requires the use of Autogen to implement the reflection pattern, which led me to explore OpenAIChatCompletionClient class of Autogen library.

I tried using OpenAIChatCompletionClient by passing base_url and token which returned 404 resource not found, since all SAP AI Core requests redirect to Azure OpenAI platform that requires api_version as  a mandatory query parameter. But OpenAIChatCompletionClient class doesn't provides api_version to the LLM while calling it. After further deep dive I figured out AzureOpenAIChatCompletionClient, which allows requests to be redirected successfully via SAP AI Core to Azure OpenAI by passing the api_version.

Reason to use AzureOpenAIChatCompletionClient

  • OpenAIChatCompletionClient cannot be used directly.

  • Instead, we need:

    • base_url: The model deployment URL from SAP AI Core.

    • api_key: A bearer token from GenAI Hub (via SAP AI Core).

    • api_version: Required by Azure for all chat completions.

    • Additional headers like AI-Resource-Group.

Code Sample

Below code sample shows how we can get deployment url and auth token using generative-ai-hub-sdk, that can be passed to AzueOpenAIChatCompletionClient along with api_version.

from autogen_agentchat.agents import AssistantAgent
from autogen_ext.models.openai import AzureOpenAIChatCompletionClient
from autogen_core.models import ModelFamily
from gen_ai_hub.proxy import GenAIHubProxyClient
from ai_core_sdk.ai_core_v2_client import AICoreV2Client
from autogen_agentchat.ui import Console
import asyncio

# Fetch deployment URL from SAP AI Core
def get_deployment_url():
    try:
        ai_core_client = AICoreV2Client.from_env()
        resources = ai_core_client.deployment.query(resource_group="default", scenario_id="foundation-models").resources
        return resources[0].deployment_url if resources else None
    except Exception:
        return None

# Fetch token and prepare model client
base_url = get_deployment_url()
gen_ai_hub_proxy_client = GenAIHubProxyClient(resource_group='default')
token = gen_ai_hub_proxy_client.get_ai_core_token().replace("Bearer ", "")

model_client = AzureOpenAIChatCompletionClient(
    model="gpt-4.1",
    base_url=base_url,
    api_key=token,
    default_headers={'AI-Resource-Group': 'default'},
    model_info={
        "family": ModelFamily.GPT_41,
        "vision": False,
        "function_calling": True,
        "json_output": False
    },
    api_version="2023-05-15" // api_version as per SAP AI Core documentation
)

# Tool example
async def get_weather(city: str) -> str:
    return f"The weather in {city} is 73 degrees and Sunny."

# Define the agent
agent = AssistantAgent(
    name="weather_agent",
    model_client=model_client,
    tools=[get_weather],
    system_message="You are a weather assistant. Give complete details about the weather in the city requested by the user."
)

# Run the agent
async def main() -> None:
    await Console(agent.run_stream(task="What is the weather in New York?"))
    await model_client.close()

asyncio.run(main())

Conclusion

Above example is referred from Autogen documentation. For api_version, ChatCompletionClient can be referred from SAP AI Core documentation.  Output from above code:

---------- TextMessage (user) ----------
What is the weather in New York?
---------- ToolCallRequestEvent (weather_agent) ----------
[FunctionCall(id='call_WJfl63g7skHT3dWnxMCwNtCr', arguments='{"city":"New York"}', name='get_weather')]
---------- ToolCallExecutionEvent (weather_agent) ----------
[FunctionExecutionResult(content='The weather in New York is 73 degrees and Sunny.', name='get_weather', call_id='call_WJfl63g7skHT3dWnxMCwNtCr', is_error=False)]
---------- ToolCallSummaryMessage (weather_agent) ----------
The weather in New York is 73 degrees and Sunny.

I hope this post helps other teams building solutions on SAP AI Core to successfully integrate newer versions of AutoGen to call the LLM and build multiple AI agents.

Elevate with AI !!

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud - QRC3 Snowflake Key-Pair Authentication Update, New Data Sources
entry.content:
Update Overview

QRC3 brings a few important updates in SAP Analytics Cloud in the Connectivity area.

  • Support for Snowflake Key-Pair authentication
  • Support for Oracle Exadata 19c, Oracle 19c, Oracle 20c, PostgreSQL 14 and PostgreSQL15
    • The new versions of the data sources will be supported

Who should read this article?

  • SAP Analytics Cloud Administrators who manage the connectivity to SAC
  • If you have been using Generic JDBC to import from Oracle or PostgreSQL
  • If you are using the existing Snowflake connection with username and password

Snowflake Key-Pair Authentication More Details

Snowflake is blocking single factor authentication November 2025 (https://www.snowflake.com/en/blog/blocking-single-factor-password-authentification/). This means that SAP Analytics Cloud Import Connections using username and password would fail. 

What do I need to do?

Step 1) Configure Snowflake Key-Pair Authentication

For a more steps from Snowflake, go here

  1. Generate Private Key
    1. Open a Terminal window
    2. You can generate an encrypted or unencrypted private key 

      unencrypted:
      openssl genrsa 2048 | openssl pkcs8 -topk8 -inform PEM -out rsa_key.p8 -nocrypt
      encrypted: 
      openssl genrsa 2048 | openssl pkcs8 -topk8 -v2 des3 -inform PEM -out rsa_key.p8

      These commands will generate a file in PEM format:

      -----BEGIN ENCRYPTED PRIVATE KEY-----
MIIE6T...
-----END ENCRYPTED PRIVATE KEY-----
    3. This file should be saved into the SAC Agent > Tomcat directory. For example, it can be saved into  the .../tomcat/webapps/ directory
  2. Generate Public Key

    1. In the Terminal Window, generate the public key based on the private key above

      openssl rsa -in rsa_key.p8 -pubout -out rsa_key.pub
    2. The command will generate a public key in PEM format

      -----BEGIN PUBLIC KEY-----
MIIBIj...
-----END PUBLIC KEY-----
  3. Assign Public Key to Snowflake User
    1. In Snowflake, run the following command to assign the public key to a Snowflake user. This snowflake user that imports the data from SAC.

      ** Note:
      - You must have MODIFY PROGRAMMATIC AUTHENTICATION METHODS or OWNERSHIP privileges on the user. or SECURITYADMIN role or higher.. 
      - copy the full public key from the rsa_key.pub file **

      ALTER USER example_user SET RSA_PUBLIC_KEY='MIIBIjANBgkqh...';

 

Step 2) Restart SAC Agent
Step 3) Create / Update a Snowflake Connection
  1.  Go to Connections > SQL Databases 

     

  2. Select the SAC Agent location and the Snowflake Connection Type. For instructions on setting up the JDBC drivers, go here: 
  3. Configure the Snowflake connection

    Note: The major change in QRC3 is we are accepting the private_key_file parameter in this input parameter. This will reflect the location of the private key stored on the SAC Agent (deployed on Tomcat) resides. 


  4. For the Username, please use the same Snowflake user that he public key is associated with in the previous section.

    The passphrase area is used ONLY if you are using an encrypted private key. If you are not using an encrypted private key, you can leave this blank. 

  5. Save this connection.
  6. You are done!

Please note if you are modifying an existing connection, you will have to edit the existing connection, modify and resave. 

Feel free to send me a DM if you have any specific questions

Thank you all for your time and have a great day! 

Best Regards,
Jimmy

 

 

 

 

Q&A

entry.title: Re: CAN'T EDIT BANK STATEMENT
entry.content:
Dear All,

When the bank statement is created via web service, it is only possible to edit/update the below fields:

  • Validity Year

  • Posting Date

  • Document Description

  • Closing Balance Amount

  • Total Credit Amount

  • Total Debit Amount

  • Items Total Number Value

Refer to the below Help Documentation (Section: Update scenario) for additional details, please.

https://help.sap.com/doc/a2b4c7ef61d64397b2b1d052082a8a08/2505/en-US/PSM_ISI_R_II_FOP_MANAGE_BANK_STMNT_IN.html

Regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Intermittent Initial Load Slowness for Random T-Codes Affecting Random Users
entry.content:
Dear SAP BASIS/HANA Experts,

In our S/4 HANA production environment, we are encountering intermittent initial load slowness for various transaction codes (T-codes), affecting different users on a random basis. The issue occurs when a user executes a T-code for the first time during the day. Subsequent executions generally respond promptly. However, the issue may reoccur for the same or different users with different T-codes on the following days.

Our observations and findings are as follows:

  • The behavior is unpredictable, affecting random users and random T-codes.

  • The slowness is temporary, typically lasting 30–40 seconds, occasionally up to 1–2 minutes.

  • By the time the issue is reported, it has usually resolved, making it difficult to activate traces or collect dumps.

  • Analysis of STAD data reveals very high DB request times (sequential reads) during slowness against the affected user.

  • Affected users appear to be stuck in the “DYNPLOAD” action, as observed in SM50.

  • Program, screen buffer, table buffer parameters value are sufficient in ST02, No SWAP.

System Environment:

  • Standard architecture: S/4 application and HANA database hosted on the same server

  • Total RAM: 2 TB

  • HANA Memory Allocation Limit: 1600 GB

  • SAP Application Memory: 180 GB

  • Recent upgrade from 1.5 TB to 2 TB RAM completed prior to issue onset

We would appreciate your inputs and recommendations on how best to diagnose and address this behavior, particularly given the inconsistency and unpredictable nature of the symptoms.

Looking forward to your expert guidance.

entry.title: Re: SLD does not update the company list even though the...
entry.content:
Dear nidhisingh14,

Thank you very much for your response and support.

This is what my SAP partner responded in two separate conversations (separated by dotted lines):

------------------------------------------------

Lola León

Dear Ricardo, good morning,

What you suggest can be done; however, doing so would mean being left without SAP and having to reconnect or reinstall the add-ons.

We can proceed with your confirmation and under your responsibility, since, as previously communicated, we do not have the installers to ensure a recovery point, nor could we download a new license package.

Best regards.

--------------------------------------------------

Jacinto Pastor

Hi Ricardo, as Lola mentioned, the process you indicated was not carried out because we do not have a guaranteed rollback point.

Removing the registration of the main SAP service from the SLD portal means that the SAP client will not be able to complete the connection while the removal and re-registration process is taking place.

Additionally, while this does not result in the loss of users or licenses (since the hardware key does not change), it does affect the operational continuity of services during the removal and re-registration of the SLD.

Because we do not have a guaranteed rollback point, performing this test under these conditions exceeds what is considered best practice. That is why it has not been executed. To ensure proper service, an update scenario was suggested instead, since we do have SAP and add-on installers available in that case.

I hope this helps clarify the scope of what Lola mentioned.

Best regards.

-------------------------------

Could you please confirm whether their explanation is accurate or if it’s safe to proceed with removing and re-registering the server in the SLD?

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: Versions in SAP Service Asset Manager
entry.content:
This is probably way more information than you really wanted related to versioning but unfortunately there is no one single version number to focus on. When talking versions, you need to know what component you are looking at.

To start with, for SSAM and creating a branded client, you are concerned with these three version and which combinations are supported as per the 2933065 - SAP Service and Asset Manager - MDK Support Matrix.

ComponentExampleDescription
SSAM2410Top level umbrella version number for the release. Covers the SSAM Metadata and Branding SDK
SSAM MDK Plug In2410.0.7Version of the SSAM extension controls
MDK SDK24.7.9MDK SDK Client to create local and branded clients

Once you are using the correct combination, you need to also consider the following versions.

Version NumberLocationDescription
App VersionMDKProject.jsonDefines the version for the branded client executable (APK/IPA)
Android Version CodeMDKProject.jsonUsed by Android to determine if the version of the BundleID is newer than a currently installed version.
Metadata Version (Definitions Version)Application.app
(Displayed in App Update)		Informational version number. Applications can choose to display this within the app to show the Metadata version.
Schema VersionApplication.app
(Displayed in App Update)		Defines for the Editor what MDK client version the metadata will be used with. Allows the Editor to not show newer controls/properties that are supported in the specified version.
Revision NumberDisplayed in App UpdateIncrementing number based on how many versions have been deployed. Resets to 0 if the App Update feature is removed (along with all deployed versions) and then re-added to the app in Mobile Services.

For the popup to update prompt (App Update), this is driven based on the current Revision number in Mobile Services as compared to the revision currently running on the client. If the Mobile Services Revision is greater than the current revision on the client the OnWillUpdate event is triggered in the App metadata which displays the prompt. When you first install a client (APK/IPA) on the device even if you have branded in metadata the current revision on the client is zero and if you have deployed metadata to Mobile Services it will detect the newer revision and trigger the prompt.

None of the other version numbers matter with respect to App Update. For example, the Metadata version can be the same when you deploy to Mobile Services but each subsequent deploy will result in a new Revision number and in turn trigger the App Update prompt.

Hopefully this helps clarify why you always see the App Update prompt even if the metadata you branded into the APK is the same as what is deployed to Mobile Services.

entry.title: Re: Can you prevent an order from being created when VPRS...
entry.content:
Hello @goody-1 

There’s no standard setting to block Sales Order creation based on VPRS (cost condition) being zero in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

However, this can be achieved using In-App Extensibility with a custom validation.

Recommendation

  • You can implement a check using Custom Logic (BADI: SD_SALES_DOCUMENT_CHECK) to trigger an error during Sales Order creation if VPRS is zero.

For Purchase Orders, VPRS is not directly applicable during creation, as it relates to sales pricing and cost of goods sold in SD.

Useful References

  • Custom Logic (BADI) – SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • In-App Extensibility – SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Thanks and kind regards

Chuma

We have an ECC6  customer considering Lean SDT , as the migration/conversion strategy to S/4HANA Rise Private Cloud. Our customer has expressed concerns regarding the expected effort for Customer-Vendor Integration (CVI), as they have a significant volume of customers, vendors, and project-related master data.   

The customer is asking whether it would be possible to perform master data cleanup or deletion, and CVIs within the ECC Shell Copy environment, after the Shell system is created but before starting the S/4HANA conversion.

Their logic is that, since there are no transactional data in the Shell Copy, performing this cleanup could be technically simpler and would drastically reduce the overall CVI complexity.  Is it supported and technically possible to delete or clean up master data (customers, vendors, project structures), and CVIs in the ECC Shell Copy before triggering the S/4HANA conversion process?

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Bringing federal Public Sector cloud solutions to life for SAP America
entry.content:
A new digital foundation for the US federal government

SAP provides commitment and energy to support US federal government agencies as they meet the challenges of today and innovate for tomorrow. Learn how a cross-functional Industry team demonstrated that SAP’s innovations and solutions can drive government efficiency, by creating premier content and experiences tailored to this unique time in the federal government landscape.

Meeting this moment

Today, the US government faces a digital turning point. Fragmented systems hinder the very operations that underpin national security and economic strength – from hiring and workforce management to budgeting, travel, logistics and mission delivery. These aren’t background tasks – they’re frontline levers of national competitiveness. When they fail, progress slows, costs rise, and force readiness suffers.

For a generation, SAP has been a partner to US government agencies in the Department of Defense, Federal Civilian and Security arenas. SAP’s NS2 is our independent, US national subsidiary that holds 7+ security authorizations and has earned 8 consecutive superior ratings from the Defense Counterintelligence & Security Agency – an honor held by only 5% of cleared organizations.

SAP is ready to re-write America’s future with secure, mission-ready AI. Our US-based Industry Advisory met the moment, quarterbacking a regional approach to activities ranging from brand voice and summit meetings, to partnering agreements and investments, to demand generation and network building at an industry-specific SAP NOW event in Washington DC this June 12th.

The US Industry Advisory team in collaboration with the IBU Public Sector positioned SAP America in alignment with the administration’s priorities for a secure and more efficient government. They articulated how our US investments, innovation and workforce expansion fuel American economic growth. For example, SAP America plans to double US GDP contribution from $100B to $200B by 2029, simultaneously creating 400,000 new US jobs in the SAP ecosystem.  

"SAP is built to help the largest, most complex organizations move faster, work smarter, and deliver more, while keeping safety and security first. This is our chance to build a stronger, AI-enabled, and more secure digital foundation that fuels impact today and trust for decades to come."

Michele Hovet, North American Industry Advisory, Public Services, Healthcare and Utilities 

Firing the customer imagination

SAP is the only technology company that combines decades of mission-critical experience, deep understanding of government operations, and AI-powered tools that are already trusted by 90% of the Fortune 2000. By taking the bold choice to reinvent with SAP as a trusted partner, the U.S. government can operate with the same AI-powered speed, precision, and agility as the world’s top businesses – without compromising on security, scale, or compliance.

Making vision tangible is where the SAP Lab’s SIX team comes in. You know them from the Business Unleashed Experience Journey for Business Suite. This immersive perspective imagines how customers achieve transformation using a combination of industry-specific applications, a harmonized foundation of business-ready data, and AI delivering faster time-to-value. Seizing this chance to pilot an end-to-end experience journey for the Public Sector, colleagues teamed up to produce the Federal Innovation Showcase.

Well-received at the event, this showcase follows a program manager seeking to accelerate their project within highly regulated boundaries under the oversight of a Deputy Secretary, and participation from the Chief Finance Officer, Chief Procurement Officer and Chief Human Capital Officer. Our storyline can be used in customer meetings to illustrate Agentic AI and integrated data-based insights. Viewers immediately ‘got it’ and delighted in walking across processes with the help of our AI copilot.

"The SAP Business Suite is the only comprehensive solution designed to modernize how the whole of government truly works at the operational level. Built on decades of federal experience and proven at scale (across civilian and defense), SAP supports embedded AI and automation for real-time decisions and efficiency. We can show agencies that they can act with confidence, maximize value, and respond faster – from the command center to the frontlines."

Andreas Wiedemann, SIX Portfolio Management Center of Excellence

Enduring value attainment

Real transformation requires a unified, secure, and intelligent foundation. One that can operate in real time with AI-powered insight, not after-action reporting. It must scale across government from central agencies to field operations. Security and compliance need to be embedded by design, producing results that withstand public, legislative, and mission scrutiny. This suite solution can then produce strategic awareness and support mission execution across finance, procurement, HR and logistics.

SAP’s IBU Public Sector Global Advisory team targets new and accelerated investment in solutions, environments, enablement and integrated demo capabilities that support the Public Sector customer journey onto cloud suite products. They support regions with tools that enable customer conversations, and tactics to secure each journey. The goal is to produce enduring value for the customer, and industry-leading excellence from SAP.

Called upon to help with this American challenge, IBU colleagues translated the technical and process specificity of the federal audience into a range of new assets. These include solution videos that speak to SAP’s capabilities and introduce us to the new contacts who our regional colleagues are meeting in this moment of change. Transformation at scale can be daunting: each video presents one process to show how government can hire, buy, budget, and deliver more effectively. 

"Across every mission and industry – from HR to finance and supply chain to citizen services – SAP helps government solve big problems in a big way. The challenge is real, but so is the opportunity: to modernize the digital backbone of government and unlock a new era of AI-enabled intelligence, efficiency, and mission continuity."

Martin Klein, IBU Public Sector, Global Industries and Innovation Experience 

84% of government organizations will prioritize investments in data intelligence

90B cyber events happen each week against a city like Washington DC, threatening people, data and assets

The US Administration needs systems that deliver proof, not promises

American officials aren’t short on priorities. The goals are clear: increase competitiveness, reduce waste, and deliver visible public value. SAP’s Industry teams can lead that structural change by consolidating fragmented IT into a unified, AI-powered cloud platform that both advances national readiness and the goals of the US Executive Branch.

 

SAP groups who contributed to this Industry event include: Strategy & Operation's Advisory and Marketing teams; SAP America's Industry, Customer and Solution Advisory teams; US Public Services; SAP NS2's Customer, Product, and Security & Compliance teams; SIX IBU Industry Advisors, GTM, Learning, and Showcase teams

entry.title: Re: Maintain Ship-to-party parameter in SAP PO Template
entry.content:
Hello @Dylan2 

To change your company’s address on PO print templates:

  • Update the Plant Address (SSCUI 101017) or Company Code Address, based on how the form is built.
  • In standard PO forms, $.ShipToParty comes from the Plant Address.
  • If the form uses custom fields, check the CDS View or Form Data Source to confirm the source.

Thank you and kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: Candidate and Manager need to sign Offer Letter (without DocuSign nor AdobeSign)
entry.content:
We have a requirement to allow Manager to be the first recipient of offer letter for their approval by them providing their initial and date in the letter before sending to the candidate. Then the candidate also needs to provide their initial and date when accepting the offer.

Currently we cannot find a solution to allow this requirement. Our organization do not approve of AdobeSign nor Docusign to integrate as they only prefer to have the candidate/manager enter their name/date stamp in the document instead.

entry.title: Re: is it possible to get the payment medium flat file in...
entry.content:
Hello @Kavitha_141 

 A clean "table view" output is not standard but can be designed with a custom format or handled outside the system.

Options;

You can develop a Custom Payment Medium Format using the Manage Payment Formats app, where you structure the file closer to your needs (e.g., CSV with clear separators).
Alternatively, post-processing of the file externally (Example, with Excel or a script) can help present it in a readable table format.

SAP Help References:

  • Payment Medium Formats – SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Manage Payment Formats App – SAP Help(select the country)

With kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: New enhancements to RISE with SAP Methodology dashboard
entry.content:
We are excited to announce significant enhancements to the RISE with SAP Methodology dashboard, building upon the application's successful release in January this year. These new updates aim to provide a more comprehensive and insightful experience, empowering you to optimize your SAP S/4HANA system and achieving a clean core. 

**Improved Software Stack Assessment** 

The updated dashboard now offers a more robust software stack assessment, emphasizing the importance of maintaining the latest SAP S/4HANA product version or feature package stack. By ensuring your system is up-to-date, you can leverage SAP-delivered innovations, mitigate security risks, and align with the clean core principles. This enhancement enables you to:  

  • Verify that your system is on the latest SAP S/4HANA product version or feature package stack 
  • Install the latest feature package stack/support package stack to stay current with SAP innovations and security patches 

**Simplified Upgrade Readiness Check** 

Upgrading to SAP S/4HANA has never been more streamlined, thanks to the SAP Readiness Check for SAP S/4HANA Upgrades. This innovative tool empowers you to assess your current system's readiness for a seamless upgrade to your desired target SAP S/4HANA release. With a single glance, the SAP Readiness Check card provides a comprehensive overview of the most critical findings from your system's latest assessment, enabling you to gauge your preparedness for the upgrade or estimate the necessary effort. 

**Enhanced Extensibility Information** 

To facilitate a better understanding of your system's extensibility, we have introduced enhanced information on separate software components using the ABAP language version ABAP for Cloud Development. This update helps you: 

  • Determine if a separate software component has been created for building extensions in Tier 1 
  • Gain insight into your system's extensibility architecture and identify potential areas for improvement 

**Unused Customer Objects: A Key Focus Area** 

The updated dashboard now provides a dedicated card to track Unused Customer Objects, which are extensions created outside of the SAP standard. Reducing the number of Unused Customer Objects is a crucial goal of the extensibility dimension. The card displays: 

  • The current share of Unused Customer Objects in your system 
  • The number of Unused Objects and Trackable Objects in your system 

**Execution of Customer Objects: A Measure of Business Process Reliance** 

To assess your efforts for future upgrades and adaptation, the dashboard now offers a card that measures the Execution of Customer Objects. This update helps you: 

  • Understand how heavily your business processes rely on executable customer objects 
  • Compare the frequency of executable customer object execution to the total execution of all executable objects in your system (including SAP, partner, and customer objects) 

**Introducing the Operations View** 

In addition to these enhancements, we are introducing a new view.  

The RISE with SAP Operations View is a dashboard available for RISE with SAP customers designed to deliver daily insights and historical data on system health-related key performance indicators, enabling organizations to efficiently align with the clean core compliance goals from the operations dimension. 

Several metrics are collected from SAP Cloud ALM applications for all your configured, productive  SAP S/4HANA systems and evaluated on a daily basis against specific thresholds. The results of these metric compliance calculations are aggregated and organized in KPIs and scores displayed in the overview and detailed pages.  

Metrics, thresholds and evaluation algorithms are shared ensuring a homogeneous compliance calculation across all RISE with SAP customers. 

***Key Features and Benefits*** 

The Operations View provides daily insights, featuring up to 30 days of historical data available until the day before, for the System Health score and its KPIs: Connectivity, Exceptions, Background Processing and Performance. 

  • Overview of System Health score for all your productive SAP S/4HANA systems (Cloud Private Edition and On Premise) 
  • System-specific view to identify and analyze metrics deviations responsible for each KPI 
  • Jump-in to standard SAP Cloud ALM applications to access the highest level of details 

Overview page of the Operations View Dashboard 

For more information about the Operations View dashboard, see https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-alm/applicationhelp/operations-view on the SAP Help Portal and the “Introducing the new RISE with SAP Operations View Dashboard” on the SAP Community Blogs portal. 

These updates demonstrate our ongoing commitment to delivering a comprehensive and actionable RISE with SAP Methodology dashboard. By leveraging these enhancements, you can further optimize your SAP S/4HANA system, achieve a clean core, and drive business success. More details can be found on our Help Portal page: https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-alm/applicationhelp/rise-with-sap. 

If you don’t want to miss out any new feature, subscribe to the What’s New viewer:  What's New in SAP Cloud ALM | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Re: Down payment request exceeding the PO value
entry.content:
Hello Harish, 

This is the expected system behavior. 

When a Down Payment Requests is made, the Purchase Order number is filled in as a reference to the order. The Down Payment Requests is actually in the supplier account and is not tied to the Purchase Order, so the system does not issue a warning.

We have a KBA that provides more details about this behavior. Please see the information below:

3614465 - Down Payment Requests exceeding Purchase Order value

URL: https://me.sap.com/notes/3614465

Regards, 

Ihan

entry.title: Automating UI5 testing: How to thrive with wdi5
entry.content:
Recently I built an extension app which featured a dynamic checklist for users to complete. Questions appear based on answers already given, and there are a large number of different scenarios, each with a different question set. Some questions are unique to a particular scenario, but many others are shared across scenarios.

In order to maintain my sanity I decided to set up automated testing. My concern was that changes and fixes made for one scenario could cause issues with another scenario.  With automated testing I can test all of them every time I make changes.

As I wanted to test at the UI-level I was looking for end-to-end testing, replicating a real user journey.  This is distinct from unit tests, for example, which cover a simple 'unit' like a function. 

I decided that wdi5 (pronounced vee-d-i-5) was a good fit for my requirements. It's a plugin for Webdriver.IO, a popular automation framework. Whereas Webdriver.IO is agnostic when it comes to UI technologies, wdi5 is optimised for use with SAPUI5 and OpenUI5.  It brings specific APIs which you can use to interact with your UI5 apps in your test scripts.

I thought I should share some of the things I learnt during this project. I do so not as an expert on automated testing in general or wdi5 in particular (I'm not!), but rather as experienced UI5 developer looking to make more use of automated testing in my day to day work. The perspective of the novice is different to that of subject matter experts, and the latter might not anticipate the questions and problems that the former face.

If you too are new to wdi5 you might not know that:

You can use wdi5 in SAP Business Application Studio (BAS)

wdi5 tests run in-browser. The browser could be on your own machine or it could be virtual.  If you're a BAS user you can install the Headless Testing Framework plugin and then run your wdi5 tests from within BAS. The tests are running in headless Firefox. If you take this approach you need to take care that the Firefox version listed in your Webdriver.IO config file (wdio.conf.ts) matches the one that BAS is using.  Every now and then SAP will upgrade the Firefox version in the plugin, and you will get an error stating 'Unable to find a matching set of capabilities'. When this happens just check what the new Firefox version is ("firefox -v" at the CLI) then update your config accordingly. NB you can use any browser with wdi5, I just used Firefox because it's easy to use in BAS

You might want to bail

I want to know as soon as any of my tests fail. By setting the bail property to true, execution will finish as soon as the first failure occurs.  You can set this in your config file (wdio.conf.ts).

It's easy to debug your tests

If you open the JavaScript Debug Terminal, and run your tests from there, processing will pause at the breakpoints you add in your test files. If you are using BAS you can do this from the Command Palette.

You should probably put the kettle on

My 35 tests take about an hour to run. This requires some planning, like starting them before you take your lunch break.  I did find that tweaking the tests can make a big difference.  Some wdi5 functions are faster than others.  For example, I found the getChildren method was quite slow, especially when the controls weren't visible on screen (and therefore not in the DOM). It's also possible to run tests in parallel.

 

Everything is asynchronous

In your tests the function calls are asynchronous, and you need to await each one.  This is true even if the corresponding UI5 function is synchronous e.g getText().

wdi5 ❤️ TypeScript

You can write your tests in TypeScript (i.e. as .ts not .js files). See here if you want to know why this is desirable.

You need to get scrolling

Your tests can only interact with screen controls that are shown on screen.  You need to use scrolling functions like scrollIntoView() or browser.scroll() to ensure that the control you need is visible.

It's easy to screenshot your errors

When a test fails the root cause isn't always obvious.  Was there an error?  Was the control we tried to interact with even visible on screen?  I love that you can always capture a screenshot if there was an error, so you can see exactly what the screen looked like at that moment.  I did this by calling the browser.screenshot() function in the afterTest handler.

 

If you are ready to write your first wdi5 tests, keep the points above in mind and check out these resources:

Resources

wdi5 GitHub Documentation

Use WDIO and WDI5 for testing UI5 apps

state of testing in UI5: OPA5, UIVeri5 and wdi5

UI5's unified test tech stack

Testing UI5 Apps with wdi5 - Zero to Hero to Continuous Integration
